title: lobelia hederacea var. elliptica
intro: Lobelia hederacea var. elliptica, also known as American lobelia or lobelia, is a flowering plant species that belongs to the family Campanulaceae. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma, due to its expectorant and anti-inflammatory properties. The plant's leaves and roots have been used to make herbal preparations, including teas, tinctures, and infusions, which have been shown to provide various health benefits, including reducing stress, anxiety, and inflammation, as well as promoting relaxation and improved sleep quality. Herbal preparations of lobelia can be made by drying the leaves and roots, then infusing them in hot water or mixing them with other herbs to create a tincture or salve.
